parsed_review.summary: CP00-484 is a descriptor-only RP16.P02 governance implementation-slice pack (40 units, risk B, range RP16.P02.M03.S01-RP16.P02.M04.S18) and passes independent read-only review with no findings. Verification: (1) Scope and distributions are consistent across registry.js (GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP484_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS), service.js descriptor builders, contracts/governance-core-contract.json, and the manifest — deliverable distribution (contract:2/implementation:18/security_audit:4/ui:6/claude_review:2/failure_recovery:4/test:4=40) and micro-title split (Primary Implementation Slice:22, Secondary Workflow Slice:18) match the prompt baseline exactly. (2) Implementation is pure frozen-object descriptor construction following the established CP480-483 pattern; every row sets descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, plus a comprehensive no-write/no-runtime/no-leak attestation. A grep of packages/governance/src for fs/require/child_process/fetch/exec/spawn/process.env/.write returned no matches, confirming no real data, no I/O, and no product runtime execution. (3) Tests (model.test.js) add three CP484 cases covering coverage validation, descriptor-only row guarantees, and authority-boundary/evidence-packet preservation; scripts/validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the stored contract descriptor against the in-code descriptor and asserts historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-484. (4) The pack-level production_ready claim is appropriately conservative: manifest reports production_ready:false, status:implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready:false; the Hermes production_ready_candidate:true is a local-validation signal only, not a final production or enterprise-trust claim, and post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are referenced but not yet present, which is the correct pre-review state. (5) Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor-only review/evidence packets rather than embedded product runtime. (6) Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte stdout, well-formed JSON {"ok":true}); validation-report confirms tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools, prompt contains no full diff/source, source_inspection_mode read_tools_used; none of the rejected evidence shapes (auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing fields) are present. The README-noted CP00-483 P3 remediation (next-micro handoff label) is consistently applied across service.js, validators.js, and the contract JSON. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (LDIP result, workbook docs, docx) are correctly partitioned as out-of-scope and not attributed to this pack. Final human approval remains required outside this receipt for any enterprise trust claim; Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer.
